text: Here we present robotic abdominoperineal resection for early low erectile cancer using Sennhans Transenterix robotic platform. Fifty-two years female patient was presented to our clinic complaining of rectal bleeding. Clonoscopy showed three-centimeter tumor just below the dentate line. On histology, G-one-two cancer was proven. Abdominal and thorax CT scan showed no metastasis. Magnetic resonance showed T-two-N-O cancer.
The surgeon performs medial collateral mobilization, followed by high ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and inferior mesenteric vein (IMV).
The surgeon performs a total mesorectal excision, beginning at the posterior side of the avascular plane, and subsequently frees the lateral aspects of the mesorectum.
The surgeon completes the mobilization of the anterior side, finishing the total mesorectal excision upon reaching the pelvic floor muscles.
The surgeon transects the sigmoid colon using an Echelon stapler. The surgical wounds are closed, and an end colostomy is formed.
The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position for the perineal part of the procedure. The surgeon closes the anus using a purse-string suture technique.
The surgeon makes a skin incision and reaches the abdominal dissection plane at the posterior side. The lateral sides are then dissected, and the specimen is retrieved.
The surgeon mobilizes the rectum from the vagina, then closes the wound using interrupted 3-0 Vicryl sutures.
The complete Total Mesorectal Excision (TME) specimen is displayed, showcasing the excised tissue.
